summary: Applied History Lab is presented as a portfolio infrastructure and source-system page.
final_use_case: It shows the utility of Applied History Lab by tying the project purpose to the mechanism that makes it inspectable, reusable, or professionally valuable.

instruments.instrument_name: Applied History Lab Extraction And Manifest Layer
instruments.input: files, tables, text, screenshots, source notes, and provenance markers
instruments.operation: George and Bennett. Loads case-study design, process tracing, and theory-development discipline. Allison and Zelikow. Loads decision-model plurality: rational actor, organizational process, and bureaucratic politics. Jervis. Loads perception, misperception, signaling, and adversary interpretation risks. Schelling. Loads coercion, bargaining, deterrence, and strategic commitment logic. A page-family source-use record exists. Final citation strength still depends on page-specific passage review.
instruments.output: what can be safely mined from the packet as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires files, tables, text, screenshots, source notes, and provenance markers
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds what can be safely mined from the packet as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: what can be safely mined from the packet as a reviewable artifact
instruments.status: Status: native public port complete for upload and navigation; substantive source-project verification remains limited by the source lineage ceiling and the.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept files, tables, text, screenshots, source notes, and provenance markers and produce what can be safely mined from the packet as a reviewable artifact.
instruments.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (files, tables, text, screenshots, source notes...) produces output (what can be safely mined from the packet...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.
